docs.title: 🏠 Welcome to my Niri Config
docs.text: A scrollable-tiling Wayland compositor setup on Arch Linux, configured using the KDL markup language. This setup features a unique Dual Waybar layout to perfectly accommodate 11 workspaces with custom icons, alongside comprehensive system metrics.

docs.title: ✨ Highlights
docs.text:
• Dual Waybar Setup: A centered top bar for system info and a floating bottom dock for 11 icon-based workspaces.
  • Catppuccin Macchiato: Themed with Mauve active borders and matching Waybar CSS.
  • Extensive Window Rules: Pre-configured floating, sizing, and opacity rules for apps like Floorp, OBS, and Kitty.
  • XWayland Satellite: Seamlessly handles X11 applications via xwayland-satellite.

docs.title: 📸 Gallery
docs.text: Here are various screenshots of the Niri setup in action, showcasing the dual Waybar and scrollable tiling.

docs.title: 🛠️ Installation
docs.text: This configuration relies on specific directory structures and a quirk with how Niri handles Waybar configurations.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Core: - niri - xwayland-satellite (For X11 app support) - waybar - swaync (Notification center)

Utilities & Daemons: - awww (Wallpaper daemon) - hyprlock (Screen lock, triggered on lid close) - polkit-gnome (Authentication agent) - brightnessctl & wpctl (Hardware control)

docs.title: ⚠️ The Waybar Quirk
docs.text:
Unlike Hyprland or River, Niri is stubborn about Waybar paths. It strictly fetches the Waybar config from ~/.config/waybar, not from a custom subdirectory.

Because this setup uses a dual Waybar (Top status + Bottom dock), your ~/.config/waybar directory must contain: - config & style.css (Top Bar) - config-bottom & style-bottom.css (Bottom Dock) - color.css & macchiato.css (Theme includes)

docs.title: Deploying the Config
docs.text:
1. Clone the repository:

    git clone https://codeberg.org/WgpArch/niri.git ~/.dotfiles/niri

  2. Symlink the Niri configuration:

    ln -sf ~/.dotfiles/niri/configs/niri ~/.config/niri

  3. Symlink the Waybar configuration (Crucial step!):

    ln -sf ~/.dotfiles/niri/configs/waybar ~/.config/waybar

  4. Ensure scripts are executable:

    chmod +x ~/.config/niri/scripts/*

docs.title: ⌨️ Keybinds
docs.text: Here are the default keybindings for this Niri setup. The main modifier key is Mod (Super/Windows key).

docs.title: 🖥️ Programs & System
docs.text: Keybind Action Mod + T Open Terminal (lxterminal) Mod + A Open App Launcher (Rofi) Mod + X Open Power Menu (Rofi) Mod + E Open File Manager (pcmanfm) Mod + Q Close focused window Mod + V Quit Niri Super + Alt + L Lock Screen (hyprlock)

docs.title: 📸 Screenshots
docs.text: Keybind Action Mod + Shift + S Screenshot (Custom script) Mod + Ctrl + S Screenshot interactive selection (grim/slurp) Mod + P Screenshot entire screen Alt + P Screenshot selected area Ctrl + P Screenshot active window

docs.title: 🔍 Focus & Movement
docs.text: Keybind Action Mod + Arrow Keys Focus column/window Mod + Home / End Focus first/last column Mod + Ctrl + Left/Right Focus workspace up/down Mod + Shift + Arrow Keys Move column/window

docs.title: 🗂️ Workspaces
docs.text: Keybind Action Mod + [1-0] Focus workspace 1-10 Mod + Alt + [1-0] Move window to workspace 1-10 Mod + Wheel Scroll Cycle workspaces up/down

docs.title: 📐 Layout & Sizing
docs.text: Keybind Action Mod + R Switch preset column width Mod + Shift + R Switch preset window height Mod + M Maximize column Mod + C Center column Mod + Minus / Equal Set column width -5% / +5% Mod + Shift + Minus / Equal Set window height -5% / +5% Mod + BracketLeft / Right Consume/Expel window left/right Mod + Comma / Period Consume/Expel window into/from column Mod + W Toggle window floating Mod + K Switch layout

docs.title: 🔊 Media Keys
docs.text: Keybind Action Volume Up/Down Volume 5% +/- (wpctl) Shift + Volume Up/Down Volume 1% +/- (Fine control) Mute Toggle audio mute Brightness Up/Down Backlight 5% +/- (brightnessctl)

docs.title: 🎨 Window Rules & Theming
docs.text: Niri uses the KDL configuration language, allowing for highly readable and structured window rules. This setup includes extensive rules for a seamless desktop experience.

docs.title: Look & Feel
docs.text:
• Layout: Scrollable Tiling
  • Gaps: 7px
  • Border Width: 2px
  • Active Border: #cba6f7 (Catppuccin Mauve)
  • Inactive Border: #6c7086 (Catppuccin Surface2)
  • Focus Ring: Disabled (Uses border instead)
  • Window Corners: 8px radius, clipped to geometry.
  • Default Opacity: 0.95 (Slight transparency)

docs.title: Window Rules Highlights
docs.text:
Window rules are defined in config.kdl to automatically handle sizing, floating, and opacity for specific applications:

  • Browsers (Floorp, Brave): Set to a fixed wide width (1425px) to prevent jittering.
  • Media/OFFICE (OBS, OnlyOffice): Set to a fixed comfortable width (1256px).
  • Floating Apps: Seahorse, Galculator, NM-connection-editor, Xarchiver, Pavucontrol, Stacer, and Rofi automatically open floating.
  • Kitty tty-clock: Opens tiled but sized to 30% and positioned top-right, acting like a desktop widget.

docs.title: Input Devices
docs.text:
• Keyboard: Layout us with ctrl:nocaps (Caps Lock acts as Ctrl). Repeat rate 30, delay 350.
  • Touchpad: Tap-to-click, natural scrolling, flat acceleration, two-finger scroll.
  • Mouse: Adaptive acceleration, middle emulation enabled.
  • Cursor: Adwaita theme, size 25. Hides after 5 seconds of inactivity.

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: The config sets strict Wayland-first environment variables for Qt, GTK, SDL, and Clutter apps, forcing them to use Wayland natively while X11 apps fall back to xwayland-satellite (Display :1).

docs.title: 📊 Waybar & Scripts
docs.text: Because a single bar couldn't comfortably fit 11 workspaces alongside system metrics on a standard laptop screen, this setup uses a Dual Waybar approach, launched directly from config.kdl.

docs.title: Top Bar (Status Bar)
docs.text:
• Config: ~/.config/waybar/config & style.css
  • Position: Top Center
  • Modules: The top bar uses only modules-center to create a clean, floating aesthetic.
  • Arch Linux Launcher, Clock, Weather, Tray, Temperature, CPU, Memory, Pulseaudio, Battery, Backlight, Network, Notifications, Power Profiles, Power Button.

docs.title: Bottom Dock (Workspace Bar)
docs.text:
• Config: ~/.config/waybar/config-bottom & style-bottom.css
  • Position: Bottom Center with 300px left/right margins, creating a floating "dock" effect.
  • Modules: niri/workspaces only.
  • Icons: Workspaces are mapped to Nerd Font icons based on intended use:
WS Icon Intended App 1 󰈹 Browser 2  Mail 3 󰎆 Discord 4  Spotify 5 󰖣 Chat 6 󰧮 Games 7 󰎄 Video 8 󰝚 Music 9  Youtube 10 󰎈 Photos 11  Files

docs.title: 📜 Key Scripts
docs.text:
Located in ~/.config/niri/scripts/:

Script Description screenshot_niri.sh Custom screenshot utility. launcher.sh Rofi application launcher wrapper. powermenu.sh Rofi power/logout menu wrapper. weather.py Fetches weather data for the Waybar module.
